perturbation.original_text: The Board Member will not use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose other than the performance of his or her duties as a Board Member of the HNBA unless and until the HNBA expressly authorizes the disclosure in writing.
perturbation.changed_text: The Board Member may use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, even without express authorization from the HNBA.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in section 3 strictly prohibits disclosure of Confidential Information to any third party without explicit written authorization from HNBA. The modified text removes this restriction, stating the Board Member MAY use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, even without express authorization from the HNBA. This creates direct contradictory terms, creating lack of clarity.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Board Member agrees that the Confidential Information he or she receives may be used for purposes beyond the benefit of the HNBA Board Work, including personal use, and that all rights to the proprietary and novel features contained in the Confidential Information are reserved by the disclosing Party. The Board Member may use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, even without express authorization from the HNBA. Board Member specifically agrees not to use any personally identifiable information provided by or on behalf of the HNBA, its contractors, affiliates, vendors, sponsors or employees, for any direct marketing and not to transfer such information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text prohibited the Board Member from disclosing Confidential Information without written authorization. The changed text explicitly allows the Board Member to disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, even without authorization. This directly contradicts the earlier restriction, creating a clear in-text contradiction as defined by Structural Flaws - In Text Contradiction. The contradicted text includes the changed text as well as a clause to not use any personally identifiable information, which could also be shared given the changed text, causing a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: Board Member specifically agrees not to use any personally identifiable information provided by or on behalf of the HNBA, its contractors, affiliates, vendors, sponsors or employees, for any direct marketing and not to transfer such information to any third party.
perturbation.changed_text: Board Member may use personally identifiable information provided by or on behalf of the HNBA, its contractors, affiliates, vendors, sponsors or employees, for direct marketing and may transfer such information to any third party.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in section 3 explicitly prohibits using personally identifiable information for direct marketing or transferring it to third parties. The modified text directly contradicts this by stating the Board Member MAY use the information for direct marketing and MAY transfer it to third parties. This conflict would be difficult to enforce and creates an in text contradiction.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Board Member agrees that the Confidential Information he or she receives may be used for purposes beyond the benefit of the HNBA Board Work, including personal use, and that all rights to the proprietary and novel features contained in the Confidential Information are reserved by the disclosing Party. The Board Member may use or disclose Confidential Information to any third party for any purpose, even without express authorization from the HNBA. Board Member may use personally identifiable information provided by or on behalf of the HNBA, its contractors, affiliates, vendors, sponsors or employees, for direct marketing and may transfer such information to any third party.
perturbation.justification: The original text explicitly prohibits the use of personally identifiable information for direct marketing and transfer to third parties, whereas the changed text explicitly states that the Board Member MAY use the information for direct marketing and transfer it to third parties. This directly contradicts the initial prohibition. The contradicted text further states this use is permissible, thus the change introduces a strong in-text contradiction, fitting the definition of a structural flaw where the clause is placed in multiple locations with conflicting implications.

perturbation.original_text: The Board Member agrees to restrict dissemination of Confidential Information to those persons employed by the Board Member (or the Board Member’s employer) who require access to the Confidential Information so as to assist the Board Member in carrying out his or her duties to the HNBA and then only if such personnel has a clear understanding of the confidentiality obligations imposed by this agreement and also agrees to maintain the confidentiality of the Confidential Information in accordance with the terms hereof by signing this Agreement before any Confidential Information is disclosed.
perturbation.changed_text: The Board Member is not required to restrict dissemination of Confidential Information and can share it with anyone, regardless of their understanding of confidentiality obligations or whether they have signed this Agreement.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in section 4 mandates restricting dissemination of Confidential Information and states only those who have signed the agreement can have access. The modified text eliminates this restriction, stating the Board Member is not required to restrict dissemination and can share with anyone. This creates conflict as the text has contradictory terms within itself.
perturbation.contradicted_text: The Board Member is not required to restrict dissemination of Confidential Information and can share it with anyone, regardless of their understanding of confidentiality obligations or whether they have signed this Agreement. 5. The HNBA agrees that the Board Member shall not be liable for any disclosure or use of any Confidential Information if: HNBA Board of Governors Confidentiality Agreement Page 3 of 4 (a) Such Confidential Information is publicly available or later becomes publicly available other than through a breach by the Board Member of this Agreement; or (b) Such Confidential Information is lawfully obtained by the Board Member from a third- party or parties independent of the HNBA prior to the Board Member’s disclosure; (c) The Board Member can demonstrate by documentary evidence or otherwise that such Confidential Information was known to him or her prior to its disclosure to the Board Member by the HNBA or was independently developed by the Board Member prior to such disclosure for purposes unrelated to his or her service as a Board Member; or (d) The Board Member was legally required to disclose the Confidential Information, provided that the Board Member (i) provides the HNBA and/or other disclosing party with written notice within five (5) days of knowing of such legal requirement so that the HNBA has the opportunity to pursue its rights regarding such potential disclosure, and (ii) utilizes reasonable efforts to obtain reliable assurances that the person receiving such Confidential Information will maintain the confidentiality of the same.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the Board Member is not required to restrict dissemination of Confidential Information and can share it with anyone. The contradicted text repeats this same allowance. The original text however, specifies a restriction on who can be given the information. Since the Changed Text and the Contradicted Text are the same, and they contradict the original text, then there is a contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: The Agreement shall expire five (5) years from its effective date specified above unless earlier terminated by a Party by giving the other Party thirty (30) days written notice.
perturbation.changed_text: The Agreement shall be in effect perpetually and cannot be terminated by either party for any reason.
perturbation.explanation: The original text in section 9 states the agreement expires in five years unless terminated with 30 days notice. The modified text directly contradicts this, stating that the agreement is perpetually in effect and cannot be terminated. It is unclear to a reader how long this agreement would be in effect and how to terminate, if ever.
perturbation.contradicted_text: Once the purpose of the disclosure is achieved, the HNBA will instruct the Board Member to destroy all Confidential Information supplied to the Board Member by the HNBA. The HNBA will keep a copy in its files for a period of two (2) years after the completion of each Candidate’s specific request. The Agreement shall be in effect perpetually and cannot be terminated by either party for any reason. Termination or expiration of this Agreement shall not relieve the Board Member of any obligation with respect to the Confidential Information disclosed or developed hereunder prior to termination. 10. This Agreement embodies all the understandings between the Parties concerning the subject matter hereof, and merges all prior discussions and writing between them as to confidentiality of Confidential Information. Neither Party shall be bound by any conditions, warranties nor representations with respect to confidentiality of Confidential Information other than as expressly provided in this Agreement.
perturbation.justification: The changed text states that the agreement is in effect perpetually and cannot be terminated by either party for any reason, directly contradicting the original text and the contradicted text which imply the agreement has a finite duration or can be terminated. The contradiction arises because the contradicted text mentions 'Termination or expiration of this Agreement,' which is not possible according to the changed text. Therefore, there's a strong in-text contradiction regarding the agreement's duration and termination possibilities.
